Indonesian: 1. setelah  (also used in Malay to indicate consecutive actions) , 2. sesudah , selepas
bus , bis  nonstandard, influenced by Dutch pronunciation

Malay: kereta  (means carriage in Indonesian, commonly used as a shorthand for kereta api , which means train . Malay followed English derivation of car which was a contraction of horseless carriage )

tolak  (It used as in tolak peluru ( shot put ) in Indonesian for this contex, used less primarily in Indonesian to mean 'subtract', it also means 'to refuse/reject', also common meaning in Malay when used in arithmetics)
Indonesian: dorong  (means "to push" in Malay, but often used to mean "to support")
kamar  (from Dutch kamer ) , ruang  (Javanese, for storage areas etc.)
air keran  (from Dutch Kraan ) , air ledeng  (" ledeng " also means "plumbing", from Dutch " leiding ")
pasta gigi  odol  ( genericized from Odol , German toothpaste's brand)

Malay: pokok  in Indonesian means "principal" or "core" or "staple", in Sundanese  poko means authentic, e.g. authentic cuisine , pohon

Indonesian: sangat, amat, sekali, banget  (from Javanese ngoko )

Malay: anda  (very formal) , awak , kamu , engkau , kau

Indonesian: negara , negeri  in Malaysia, negeri usually refers to a state within a federation
warnet  a portmanteau for " warung internet " ( lit. internet shop)

Malay: peti sejuk  lit. cool box (rarely used in Indonesian)
Indonesian: lemari es , lemari pendingin  ( lit. cooler closet) , kulkas  (from Dutch koelkast )

Malay: berkongsi  (In Indonesian, it means "creating trading company" or "plotting something bad")

Indonesian: waktu  from Arabic الوقت  ( al-Waqt ) , masa  (can be used for 'in a specific long period of time' in Indonesian)
miliar  (from Dutch miljard ) , milyar ( informal but frequently used )
lipas , kecoa  (from Southern Min Chinese: ka-choa̍h – 虼蚻)
